task_completion.description: Open the 'Organ of Corti' sample image in ImageJ/Fiji.
task_completion.visual_evidence: ImageJ/Fiji interface showing an open image window titled 'Organ of Corti' (or similar indicating the correct sample).
A dialog box or window titled 'Directionality' is open within the ImageJ/Fiji application.

task_completion.description: Configure the Directionality tool for at least 10 angular bins and a range of at least 150 degrees.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Directionality dialog showing input fields for 'nbins' (or similar) set to 10 or higher, and a histogram range (start/end) spanning 150 degrees or more.

task_completion.description: Generate the orientation histogram and summary statistics (Preferred Direction and Dispersion).
task_completion.visual_evidence: A results window, plot, or table generated by the Directionality plugin displaying the histogram data, along with calculated values for 'Direction' (Preferred Direction) and 'Dispersion'.

task_completion.description: Save the histogram data and summary statistics to ~/ImageJ_Data/results/directionality_results.csv.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Save As' dialog showing the file being saved as 'directionality_results.csv' in the '/home/ga/ImageJ_Data/results/' directory, or a terminal/file explorer confirming the file exists at this exact path.

integrity.description: The agent used the ImageJ/Fiji graphical user interface to perform the analysis.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the actual ImageJ/Fiji application menus and dialogs, rather than just running a pre-written script in a terminal to bypass the GUI.

integrity.description: The saved CSV results originate from the actual image analysis.
integrity.visual_evidence: The values in the saved CSV or save dialog match the results displayed in the ImageJ Directionality output window, rather than being manually typed into a text editor or spreadsheet.

integrity.description: The analysis was performed on the actual Organ of Corti image.
integrity.visual_evidence: The Directionality tool was run while the 'Organ of Corti' image was the active window, not a blank, random, or incorrectly named image.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The 'Organ of Corti' image is a built-in sample in Fiji, accessible via the File > Open Samples menu. The expected output path is exactly /home/ga/ImageJ_Data/results/directionality_results.csv. The Directionality tool is a standard ImageJ plugin that calculates and displays 'Direction' (Preferred Direction) and 'Dispersion' (standard deviation) alongside the histogram data.
